steps.title: Separate the clauses
steps.content: The text first states that new polymers have been engineered to repair themselves. The next idea explains what happens upon fracture. Those two ideas need a clear sentence boundary before the phrase beginning "Upon fracture."

steps.title: Reject faulty punctuation
steps.content: Choice A creates a comma splice. Choice B uses dashes in a way that leaves the following clause improperly attached. Choice D runs the ideas together with only a comma later in the sentence. The answer is C.
